# CSA Legacy Python Analysis

This public repository preserves a legacy Python workflow for a central sleep apnea project associated with:

Locke BW, Sellman J, McFarland J, Uribe F, Workman K, Sundar KM. Predictors of Initial CPAP Prescription and Subsequent Course with CPAP in Patients with Central Sleep Apneas at a Single Center. Lung. 2023;201(6):625-634. DOI: 10.1007/s00408-023-00657-z. PMID: 37987861. PMCID: PMC10869204.

Primary final paper code repository: https://github.com/reblocke/CSA-CPAP-Prescribing

Historical preprint record: DOI 10.21203/rs.3.rs-3199807/v1; PMID 37547021; PMCID PMC10402256.

## Use

Run the legacy Python workflow with:

```bash
python DataAnalysis.py --input data/private/CSA-Db-Working.xlsm --output-dir outputs/legacy-python
```

The original patient-level workbook is restricted EHR/sleep-center data and is not public. Users must supply an authorized local workbook matching the expected historical column layout in DATA_DICTIONARY.md.

## Important Files

- README.md: human-readable overview, links, workflow, and citation guidance.
- CITATION.cff: machine-readable article and repository citation metadata.
- DATA_DICTIONARY.md: expected workbook columns, derived fields, and category mappings.
- REPRODUCING.md: local reproduction workflow with restricted data.
- DataAnalysis.py and ReadExcel.py: legacy Python source code.
- tests/fixtures/synthetic_csa_workbook.xlsx: synthetic no-PHI smoke-test workbook.

## Safety

Do not add raw clinical data, PHI, credentials, local path files, or publisher-formatted article text. Historical generated PNG/XLSX files were moved out of the default branch and archived in the legacy-python-outputs-2026-06-03 GitHub release.
